forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,22 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+depends_on:
+- 01KT9JV0N1RF0MMEBRZV4A6F3J
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: bd80
+project: command-events
+title: Route board lifecycle onto the bridge (board opened/switched/closed)
+---
+Make board lifecycle observable on the bridge (the "window change" a kanban plugin actually cares about — each board ≈ a window).
+
+Current state: board lifecycle emits direct Tauri only — `board-opened` (apps/kanban-app/src/menu.rs:838/840, payload {path}), `board-changed` (commands.rs:1571 switch, :1598 close, empty payload), plus `ui-state-changed` kinds board_switch/board_close. No bridge notifications.
+
+## Work
+- Add `notifications/board/opened|switched|closed` (NEW methods) carrying the board path, and publish on the bridge at the board open/switch/close sites.
+- Swap the frontend `board-opened`/`board-changed` listeners to the bridge-forwarded methods; remove the direct emits once swapped.
+- Declare on the window (or app) service tool. Decide owner: board lifecycle is window-service territory (board files ↔ windows). Use #[notification] struct=payload.
+- Coverage guard.
+
+## Acceptance
+A plugin can `this.window.on("board.opened"/...)` (final names TBD); the frontend reads board lifecycle from the bridge; no direct `board-opened`/`board-changed` emit remains; declared == published.
\ No newline at end of file

-
-## Work

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -18,5 +18,8 @@
 - Declare on the window (or app) service tool. Decide owner: board lifecycle is window-service territory (board files ↔ windows). Use #[notification] struct=payload.
 - Coverage guard.

+## Sibling card (keep separate — decided 2026-06-06)
+OS **window** lifecycle (created/focused/closed) is the related-but-distinct event family — `01KT9X10QPHNENHJFA638RN3D2`. Kept separate on purpose: board lifecycle = board-file ↔ window association; window lifecycle = raw OS window events. Coordinate the window-service event-name namespace so `board.opened/switched/closed` and `created/focused/closed` don't collide and share one `#[notification] struct=payload` declaration pattern.
+
 ## Acceptance
 A plugin can `this.window.on("board.opened"/...)` (final names TBD); the frontend reads board lifecycle from the bridge; no direct `board-opened`/`board-changed` emit remains; declared == published.
\ No newline at end of file

+  text: |-
+    Picked up. Researched current state — STALE REFS corrected:
+    - `board-changed` direct emits (commands.rs switch/close) are ALREADY gone: window-container.tsx now reconciles board changes off `notifications/store/changed` (board/column stores). Nothing to remove there.
+    - Only remaining direct emit is `board-opened` in menu.rs::open_and_notify (emit_to/emit "board-opened" {path}). commands.rs:1571/1598 no longer hold board-changed emits.
+    - The MCP notification bridge + per-window forwarder already exist (commands.rs spawn_window_forwarder pumps every bridge notification to its window as a Tauri event named by the notification `method`). So publishing a McpNotification onto the board's bridge IS the path.
+
+    NAMESPACE DECISION (coordinated with sibling ^38rn3d2 raw OS-window lifecycle): board lifecycle methods are `notifications/board/opened`, `notifications/board/switched`, `notifications/board/closed` (short events opened/switched/closed under the `board` segment → plugin subscribes `this.window.on("board.opened")` etc.). The sibling's raw-window lifecycle will use `notifications/window/created|focused|closed` (segment `window`). Distinct path segments (`board/*` vs `window/*`) under the SAME window-service tool — no collision, and both share one `#[notification] struct=payload` declaration pattern in window-service operations.rs. Payload struct `BoardLifecycle { path: String }` carries only the board path (thin event, no enrichment).
+
+    Publish sites: opened → menu.rs open_and_notify; switched/closed → the switch_board/close_board callbacks in main.rs (after state.open_board / state.close_board succeed). Frontend: window-container.tsx swaps the `getCurrentWindow().listen("board-opened")` to the bridge method; add board-opened to forbidden-listener guard (board-changed already there).

   timestamp: 2026-06-24T12:03:10.620247+00:00
+- actor: claude-code
+  id: 01kvws6gg81ygg7dn3j0rmjg2m
+  text: |-
+    Implementation landed. Event names: notifications/board/opened | switched | closed (short events opened/switched/closed under the `board` segment).
+
+    Rust (window-service = owner):
+    - operations.rs: three #[notification] structs BoardOpened/BoardSwitched/BoardClosed (each {path}, struct==payload), WINDOW_NOTIFICATIONS slice, notifications() accessor, and three publish helpers board_{opened,switched,closed}_notification() built via from_declared. Coverage guard `published_board_methods_are_declared` (declared⟺raised) + method/event/payload unit tests.
+    - service.rs: build_tool_definition now passes `notifications: notifications()` so the `window` tool advertises them in _meta.
+    - lib.rs: re-exports the structs + helpers + notifications().
+    - meta_snapshot.rs (real production-path test): window_tool_meta_advertises_board_lifecycle_notifications drives the REAL WindowService::list_tools and asserts io.swissarmyhammer/notifications declares opened/switched/closed with their wire methods.
+
+    Publish sites (kanban-app):
+    - opened: menu.rs::open_and_notify — replaced the direct emit_to/emit("board-opened",{path}) with: rebind the window's forwarder onto the new board's bridge (bind_window_forwarder, now pub(crate)) then publish_board_lifecycle(state,label,board_opened_notification(path)). New commands.rs helper publish_board_lifecycle resolves the window bridge (resolve_window_bridge) and publishes. Boardless/global fallback publishes on the global host bridge.
+    - switched: state.rs::touch_if_already_open — re-opening an already-open board publishes board_switched_notification on the board's bridge (new AppState::board_bridge helper: per-board host else global, mirrors start_notification_fanin).
+    - closed: state.rs::close_board — publishes board_closed_notification BEFORE the handle/bridge is dropped.
+
+    Frontend swap:
+    - mcp-notifications.ts: BOARD_OPENED/SWITCHED/CLOSED_EVENT constants, BoardLifecycle payload type, subscribeBoard{Opened,Switched,Closed} public lazy seams (for plugins/MCP clients).
+    - window-container.tsx: swapped getCurrentWindow().listen("board-opened") → statically-imported listen(BOARD_OPENED_EVENT) (direct listen for synchronous registration per the focus-twin guidance, avoids chromium flake; subscribe* stays the public seam). Removed the legacy window listener.
+    - no-tauri-change-listeners.node.test.ts: added "board-opened" to FORBIDDEN_EVENTS (board-changed already there).
+    - window-container.test.tsx: updated the two board-opened tests to the bridge event; removed now-unused emitWindowEvent helper.
+
+    Real-pipeline test (TDD RED verified — broke method name, watched it fail, restored): state.rs::board_switch_and_close_publish_lifecycle_notifications opens a board, subscribes to its bridge, re-opens (switched) and closes (closed), asserting the declared notifications + path arrive.
+
+    Verification: cargo nextest -p swissarmyhammer-window-service 37/37; -p kanban-app (not ai_panel_e2e) 189/189; cargo fmt clean; clippy clean on touched files (pre-existing warnings in shell.rs:437 + menu.rs:1406 test block + focus crate are NOT my files); tsc clean; vitest window-container.test.tsx 10/10 x5 (no flake), guard tests pass.

     Verification: cargo nextest -p swissarmyhammer-window-service 37/37; -p kanban-app (not ai_panel_e2e) 189/189; cargo fmt clean; clippy clean on touched files (pre-existing warnings in shell.rs:437 + menu.rs:1406 test block + focus crate are NOT my files); tsc clean; vitest window-container.test.tsx 10/10 x5 (no flake), guard tests pass.
   timestamp: 2026-06-24T12:20:38.024454+00:00
+- actor: claude-code
+  id: 01kvwsm2e7zd0fr67vs2cwp3bn
+  text: |-
+    NAMESPACE CORRECTION (from adversarial double-check). My earlier namespace note assumed distinct path SEGMENTS (board/* vs window/*) were enough. They are NOT: the notifications `_meta` tree (generate_notifications_meta in swissarmyhammer-operations/src/schema.rs) is keyed by the SHORT event name = the method's LAST path segment, and `this.<server>.on(event)` resolves by direct lookup on that key (sdk/plugin.ts resolveNotificationMethod → notificationsOf(tool)?.[event]). A duplicate key silently OVERWRITES (tree.insert). So the DEFAULT short event for notifications/board/closed would be "closed" — which would COLLIDE with the sibling card ^38rn3d2's planned notifications/window/closed (also default short "closed"), making one of them unsubscribable.
+
+    FIX APPLIED: each board notification now declares an EXPLICIT two-segment short event via #[notification(event = "board.opened"|"board.switched"|"board.closed")]. So a plugin subscribes `this.window.on("board.opened", …)` exactly as the task acceptance wording requires, and the tree keys are board.opened/board.switched/board.closed.
+
+    SIBLING CARD ^38rn3d2 MUST mirror this: declare its raw OS-window lifecycle with explicit short events `window.created` / `window.focused` / `window.closed` (event = "window.created" etc.), NOT the bare created/focused/closed. Then `window.closed` and `board.closed` are distinct keys in the shared `window` tool's flat _meta — no collision. The wire METHODS (notifications/board/* vs notifications/window/*) were already distinct; only the short _meta key needed the override.
+
+    Updated: operations.rs (3 event= overrides + unit-test assertions board.opened/switched/closed), meta_snapshot.rs (leaf keys board.*). Wire methods unchanged → no frontend/kanban-app change. window-service 37/37 still green. Adversarial double-check returned PASS (its only finding was this forward hazard, now fixed).
+  timestamp: 2026-06-24T12:28:02.375382+00:00
 depends_on:
 - 01KT9JV0N1RF0MMEBRZV4A6F3J
 position_column: doing

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -72,4 +72,11 @@
 OS **window** lifecycle (created/focused/closed) is the related-but-distinct event family — `01KT9X10QPHNENHJFA638RN3D2`. Kept separate on purpose: board lifecycle = board-file ↔ window association; window lifecycle = raw OS window events. Coordinate the window-service event-name namespace so `board.opened/switched/closed` and `created/focused/closed` don't collide and share one `#[notification] struct=payload` declaration pattern.

 ## Acceptance
-A plugin can `this.window.on("board.opened"/...)` (final names TBD); the frontend reads board lifecycle from the bridge; no direct `board-opened`/`board-changed` emit remains; declared == published.
\ No newline at end of file
+A plugin can `this.window.on("board.opened"/...)` (final names TBD); the frontend reads board lifecycle from the bridge; no direct `board-opened`/`board-changed` emit remains; declared == published.
+
+## Review Findings (2026-06-24 06:29)
+
+### Warnings
+- [ ] `apps/kanban-app/src/state.rs:680` — Public function `board_bridge` lacks documentation. As a newly added public(crate) async method returning a NotificationBridge, it should document its purpose, parameters, and return value so callers understand when and how to use it. Add a doc comment explaining the function's purpose. Example: `/// Get the notification bridge for the given board handle, falling back to the global host bridge if the board has no per-board platform.`.
+- [ ] `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/components/window-container.tsx:510` — `refresh()` is called without awaiting or catching its promise. If `refresh()` rejects, the rejection is unhandled. The async handler returns a Promise that will resolve successfully even if `refresh()` fails, leaving an unhandled promise rejection. Change line 510 to `await refresh().catch(() => {});` to ensure the handler waits for refresh to complete and handles any rejection, matching the try-catch pattern on line 507 and the explicit `.catch()` pattern used at line 357. (Reviewer note: line number appears misattributed — line 510 is a conditional expression in `reconcileBoards`, not a `refresh()` call; confirm the actual unawaited-promise site, likely inside the new board-opened bridge listener block, before acting.)
+- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-window-service/src/service.rs:164` — The `call_tool` method is 75+ lines with 15 repetitive deserialize-and-call match arms; a candidate for a dispatch helper/table. (Reviewer note: OUT OF SCOPE / pre-existing — this task's only service.rs change was adding the import + `notifications: notifications()` one-liner in `build_tool_definition`; `call_tool` was not touched by this diff.)
\ No newline at end of file

--- original
+++ modified
@@ -72,11 +72,4 @@
 OS **window** lifecycle (created/focused/closed) is the related-but-distinct event family — `01KT9X10QPHNENHJFA638RN3D2`. Kept separate on purpose: board lifecycle = board-file ↔ window association; window lifecycle = raw OS window events. Coordinate the window-service event-name namespace so `board.opened/switched/closed` and `created/focused/closed` don't collide and share one `#[notification] struct=payload` declaration pattern.

 ## Acceptance
-A plugin can `this.window.on("board.opened"/...)` (final names TBD); the frontend reads board lifecycle from the bridge; no direct `board-opened`/`board-changed` emit remains; declared == published.
-
-## Review Findings (2026-06-24 06:29)
-
-### Warnings
-- [ ] `apps/kanban-app/src/state.rs:680` — Public function `board_bridge` lacks documentation. As a newly added public(crate) async method returning a NotificationBridge, it should document its purpose, parameters, and return value so callers understand when and how to use it. Add a doc comment explaining the function's purpose. Example: `/// Get the notification bridge for the given board handle, falling back to the global host bridge if the board has no per-board platform.`.
-- [ ] `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/components/window-container.tsx:510` — `refresh()` is called without awaiting or catching its promise. If `refresh()` rejects, the rejection is unhandled. The async handler returns a Promise that will resolve successfully even if `refresh()` fails, leaving an unhandled promise rejection. Change line 510 to `await refresh().catch(() => {});` to ensure the handler waits for refresh to complete and handles any rejection, matching the try-catch pattern on line 507 and the explicit `.catch()` pattern used at line 357. (Reviewer note: line number appears misattributed — line 510 is a conditional expression in `reconcileBoards`, not a `refresh()` call; confirm the actual unawaited-promise site, likely inside the new board-opened bridge listener block, before acting.)
-- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-window-service/src/service.rs:164` — The `call_tool` method is 75+ lines with 15 repetitive deserialize-and-call match arms; a candidate for a dispatch helper/table. (Reviewer note: OUT OF SCOPE / pre-existing — this task's only service.rs change was adding the import + `notifications: notifications()` one-liner in `build_tool_definition`; `call_tool` was not touched by this diff.)
\ No newline at end of file
